title: Informix Stored Procedures
text: Informix Stored Procedures The INF connector generates stored procedures for the most basic I/O operations in Informix. A stored procedure is one or more SQL statements which have already been parsed and compiled. Using stored procedures provides better performance than using the equivalent SQL statements. Informix executes stored procedures immediately, because the parsing and compiling has already been done. If you generate or enter SQL statements, Informix first has to parse and compile each one before executing each statement. For more information on Informix stored procedures, see your Informix documentation. Stored Procedures Created by the INF Connector The INF connector creates and uses the stored procedures shown in the table. Table 1. Stored procedures created by INF connector Procedure name Explanation table_name _E Procedure existence check. This procedure checks if table_name has procedures. table_name _I Insert a row into table_name . This procedure is not created for tables containing segmented fields. table_name _F Fetch all nonsegmented fields in a row in table_name , for which the row matches a specific primary key and lock the row if requested. table_name _U Update a row in table_name based on either a primary key match or a primary key and U_VERSION field match. This procedure is not created for tables containing segmented fields. table_name _D Delete a row in table_name based on either a primary key match or a primary key and U_VERSION field match. table_name _S Select nonsegmented fields for all rows from table_name using the select cache. table_name _S # Select nonsegmented fields for rows from table_name using index number # ; use select cache. The connector generates a stored procedure for each index referred to in such select operations. Note: Additional stored procedures can be generated to enforce referential integrity. The connector creates stored procedures when it creates the associated base table, granting execute permissions to public on the stored procedures. Uniface can only create and execute stored procedures if you set the procs connector option in the assignment file. Segmented Fields The connector never uses stored procedures to manipulate segmented fields and the connector does not create insert or update stored procedures for tables containing segmented fields. The connector creates the other stored procedures, but these do not retrieve or modify segmented fields. The connector uses dynamic SQL to execute the relevant Read, Write, and Update requests. Serial Fields If the serial insert connector option is set on together with the procs connector option, the connector does not use the ‘insert’ stored procedure for tables that contain Serial fields. Instead, dynamic SQL is used to execute the relevant Write request. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
